question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Since there is no authentication context checked before entityRepository.findById() is called, can an unprivileged user retrieve the entity of any account including those with adminKey or scheduleKey set, and use the returned data to bypass the multi-signer authorization requirement?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user pass a base32 string that decodes to all-zero bytes to getByAliasAndType(), causing findByAlias() to match an entity with a zero-byte alias, and retrieve that entity's authorization data without any signer check?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address that is exactly 12 bytes (instead of 20) so that ByteBuffer.wrap() on the decoded bytes causes getInt() and getLong() to read zeros from uninitialized buffer positions, triggering the long-zero path and resolving to entity ID 0 without signer authorization?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Since the long-zero address branch calls entityRepository.findById(buffer.getLong()) with no ownership check, can an unprivileged user construct a long-zero EVM address encoding the ID of a schedule entity to retrieve its signers list and bypass the scheduled transaction's multi-signer authorization requirement?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that getByIdAndType() returns Optional.empty() for type mismatches (not an error) to silently probe entity types across the entire ID space without triggering any alerting, enabling systematic reconnaissance of signer-controlled entities?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the BASE32 decoder's padding behavior in decodeBase32() to produce byte arrays that match aliases of accounts they do not own, bypassing signer authorization and retrieving those accounts' entity data?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a hex-encoded EVM address with uppercase letters (since Hex.decodeHex is case-insensitive) to bypass any input sanitization and resolve to a target entity without signer verification, enabling unauthorized transaction authorization?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Since entityId.getId() is called directly with no bounds checking in getByIdAndType(), can an unprivileged user supply Long.MAX_VALUE or Long.MIN_VALUE as an entity ID to probe for integer overflow behavior in the repository layer that might return an unintended entity without signer verification?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user craft an EVM address where bytes 0-3 are zero and bytes 4-11 are zero but bytes 12-19 encode a target entity's numeric ID, exploiting the ByteBuffer sequential read pattern to resolve to that entity via findById() without any signer check?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Since getByAliasAndType() does not verify that the caller controls the private key corresponding to the alias, can an unprivileged user who knows another account's public alias retrieve that account's full entity data and use the publicKey field to construct unauthorized transactions?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any session or JWT token validation in getByIdAndType() to make unlimited unauthenticated calls to retrieve entity data for any account, enabling mass harvesting of signer key information without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a 20-byte EVM address where all bytes are zero (0x0000...0000) to trigger the long-zero path and call findById(0L), potentially retrieving a system entity (entity ID 0) without any signer authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Since the Entity returned by getByIdAndType() includes the stakedAccountId and stakedNodeId fields, can an unprivileged user retrieve staking relationships for arbitrary accounts without signer approval, enabling them to map the network's staking topology and target high-value signers?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user pass a base32 string that decodes to a byte array longer than the expected alias length, causing findByAlias() to perform a prefix match or truncated comparison that matches an unintended account, bypassing signer authorization?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Since decodeEvmAddress() uses Strings.CS.removeStart() to strip the '0x' prefix before hex decoding, can an unprivileged user supply '0x0x<hex>' to cause double-prefix stripping behavior that produces unexpected bytes, potentially matching a long-zero pattern for an unintended entity ID?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that getByIdAndType() returns the Entity's autoRenewAccountId field to discover linked accounts and then chain lookups to retrieve those accounts' signer keys without any authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a hex string of exactly 4 bytes (8 hex chars) so that ByteBuffer.wrap() creates a buffer where getInt() reads those 4 bytes and getLong() throws a BufferUnderflowException, causing an unhandled exception that leaks internal state or bypasses the type filter check?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Since the Base32 decoder in decodeBase32() does not enforce a maximum length, can an unprivileged user supply an extremely long base32 string to cause a large byte array allocation that matches no alias but consumes excessive memory, enabling a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate signers from authorizing transactions?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged user retrieve an entity with receiverSigRequired=true via getByIdAndType() and use the knowledge that this flag is set to craft a transaction that bypasses the receiver signature requirement by exploiting a downstream component that trusts the mirror node's entity data?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply an EVM address with a valid hex encoding but odd number of characters (after 0x stripping) to cause Hex.decodeHex() to throw a DecoderException that is caught and rethrown as IllegalArgumentException, leaking the raw address value in the error message and enabling information disclosure about valid vs invalid addresses?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Since getByIdAndType() exposes the Entity's proxyAccountId field without any ownership check, can an unprivileged user map proxy relationships between accounts and use this information to identify accounts whose transactions can be authorized through proxy mechanisms without direct signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that decodeBase32() silently ignores invalid base32 characters (Apache Commons Base32 is lenient) to supply a string that decodes to the alias bytes of a target account, bypassing signer authorization and retrieving that account's entity?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a long-zero EVM address encoding the ID of a token entity to retrieve that token's adminKey via the returned Entity object, enabling them to authorize token management transactions without the token owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Since the EntityRepository is a Spring Data CrudRepository with no row-level security, can an unprivileged user exploit getByIdAndType() to retrieve entities across all shards and realms (not just 0.0.x) by supplying EntityId values with non-zero shard/realm, bypassing any implicit shard-based access control?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged user supply a base32 string that decodes to the raw bytes of a known public key (used as an alias) to retrieve the associated account entity without proving they hold the corresponding private key, enabling unauthorized transaction authorization?
